signals.headline: Elon Musk Announces Step-Back from DOGE Role Amid Tesla Struggles | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Elon Musk Announces Step-Back from DOGE Role Amid Tesla Struggles | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Elon Musk confirmed he will reduce his time on the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to 1-2 days per week starting likely in May, following Tesla's 71% profit drop and political blowback from his Trump ties; Tesla stock rose 5% after-hours on the news.[1]

signals.headline: South Korea Annual Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Annual Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate rose to 2.2% in March 2026, up from 2.0% in January and February but below market forecasts of 2.4%; this is the latest confirmed data (reported around early April 2026) and remains near the Bank of Korea's 2% target amid global energy volatility.[1]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Base Rate Held at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Base Rate Held at 2.5% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
The Bank of Korea base rate was confirmed steady at 2.5% as of April 14, 2026, matching the prior level and signaling no immediate easing despite contained inflation pressures.[8]
